cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking why Babbage failed to continue his research work in 1828. The options are A to D. Looking at the passage, the third paragraph says that when Babbage returned to London in 1828, he was at the end of his resources. Many bills were unpaid, his assistant and co-workers quarreled with him and left with tools. For a year, no work was done. So the main reason here is that he ran out of money. The text mentions he was at the end of his resources, which points to financial issues. Option B is "he almost ran out of money," which matches that. Option A is about poor health, but
